Software Comparison

Fieldwire vs. Procore (2026)

Both platforms serve commercial construction — but for field teams and superintendents, Fieldwire often wins on simplicity and speed.

Fieldwire and Procore are both commercial construction platforms, but they serve very different buyers. Procore is an enterprise system covering financials, contracts, and project management end-to-end. Fieldwire is a field-first platform — plans, tasks, punch lists, and RFIs — built to be used by foremen, superintendents, and field crews, not office staff. This comparison looks at the field management angle where they most directly compete.

Quick comparison

FieldwireProcore
PricingFree–$54/user/moCustom (typically $10K+/yr)
Best forField teams, subs, foremenGCs, enterprise PM, full project lifecycle
Plan viewing⭐ ExcellentGood
Field task management⭐ Purpose-builtStrong
FinancialsNone⭐ Comprehensive
Contracts/CommitmentsNone⭐ Full module
Ease of use⭐ Very fast to learnComplex
Free plan⭐ YesNo

Fieldwire's strengths

Plan management that actually works on a job site

Fieldwire's plan viewing is its hallmark feature. You can view, annotate, and mark up construction plans on a tablet or phone — even offline. Plans sync when you're back on the network. For field crews who need to pull up drawings on site without fighting a slow connection, this is valuable.

  • Unlimited plan versions and sheet history
  • Annotation and markup tools optimized for touch screens
  • Hyperlinked sheets — tap a door to jump to the door schedule
  • Offline plan access with automatic sync
  • As-built markup and issue flagging directly on plans

Task management built for foremen

Fieldwire's task system is designed to be used by the person with boots on the ground. Create tasks linked to specific plan locations, assign to crew members or trade contractors, add photos, and track completion — all from a phone on the job site. It's faster and simpler than Procore's task tools because it's purpose-built for field use rather than being one module in a larger system.

Punch list management

Fieldwire excels at punch list management. Walk the project, flag issues on the plan, assign to the responsible party, and track through resolution — with photographic evidence at every step. This is a workflow where Fieldwire's field-first design pays off clearly.

Pricing and accessibility

Fieldwire has a free plan for teams under 5 users — genuinely functional, not crippled. Paid plans range from $29–$54/user/month. For small commercial subs who need plan management and task tracking, this is accessible pricing. Procore's minimum is typically in the $10–15K/year range for small companies.

Procore's strengths

Complete project financials

Procore's financial tools — contracts, commitments, change orders, pay applications, budget tracking — are the most complete in commercial construction. Fieldwire has none of this. For GCs who need to manage project finances end-to-end, Procore is in a different category.

Subcontractor management

Procore manages the entire subcontractor relationship: invite to bid, execute contract, manage submittals and RFIs, process change orders, approve pay apps, and collect lien waivers. Fieldwire doesn't touch this workflow.

Document control at scale

On a large commercial project with hundreds of submittals, thousands of RFIs, and extensive specification documentation, Procore's document control is more complete than Fieldwire's. The review workflows, approval chains, and audit trails matter at that scale.

Enterprise adoption

Large GCs standardize on Procore. If your major GC clients use Procore, you'll be invited to use it as a sub — which is often free for subs on specific projects. That network effect reinforces Procore's dominance in commercial construction.

When to choose Fieldwire

  • You're a specialty subcontractor or smaller GC who primarily needs field management tools
  • Budget is a real constraint — Fieldwire's pricing is dramatically lower
  • Your GC clients don't require Procore participation
  • Field crews need simple tools that get adopted without training
  • You want offline capability for remote job sites

When to choose Procore

  • You're a GC managing complex commercial projects with extensive subcontractor relationships
  • Financial management (budgets, change orders, pay apps) is as important as field management
  • Your projects require full document control, submittals, and RFI workflows
  • You're at scale where Procore's investment makes sense ($50M+ revenue)

Can you use both?

Yes — and many commercial subs do exactly this. A sub invited to Procore by their GC uses Procore for the required interactions (submittals, RFIs, pay apps) but manages their own field teams through Fieldwire, which is more user-friendly and independently controlled.

The residential contractor alternative

Neither Fieldwire nor Procore is designed for residential construction. If you're a residential contractor looking for field management and project management combined, Buildertrend and Houzz Pro are significantly better fits.

Residential contractors — Buildertrend and Houzz Pro are built for your world:
